data_sources.label: Real Proof Reports

data_sources.next_step: Import real bench, field, CI, hardware, or project proof reports with no demo/synthetic/local-proof markers.

data_sources.label: Quarantined Demo Reports
data_sources.meaning: Synthetic, demo, smoke, fixture, and local proof reports stay visible for audit but are excluded from metrics.
data_sources.next_step: Replace quarantined reports with real evidence or mark real reports explicitly with metadata.data_authenticity=real.

data_sources.label: Comparable Baseline
data_sources.meaning: A matching prior scenario separates real progress from one-off numbers.
data_sources.next_step: Repeat the same scenario after changes so every metric has a before/after comparison.

data_sources.label: Repeatability
data_sources.meaning: Multiple runs expose noise, flaky behavior, and repeated regressions.
data_sources.next_step: Run the same scenario several times before treating a change as proven.

data_sources.label: Scenario Diversity
data_sources.meaning: More scenarios reduce the risk of proving only one narrow demo path.
data_sources.next_step: Add at least one scale or field-like scenario beside the current coastal proof.

data_sources.label: Sample Scale
data_sources.meaning: Larger samples make performance, storage, and reliability claims harder to fake.
data_sources.next_step: Run proof scenarios at 100, 500, and 1000+ observations and compare the curves.

data_sources.label: AI Analyst
data_sources.meaning: Ollama reviews weak evidence, missing measurements, experiment ideas, and next actions.
data_sources.next_step: Use auto-strong for serious reviews; keep deterministic metrics as the source of truth.

data_sources.label: Project Scan
data_sources.meaning: Project profiles connect source, docs, tests, firmware, evidence, and planned metrics.
data_sources.next_step: Run plan-ai after major repo or hardware-documentation changes.

data_sources.label: Field And Bench Logs
data_sources.meaning: Power, thermal, calibration, endurance, and human acceptance data prove real-world readiness.
data_sources.next_step: Create a simple CSV or report.json path for bench and field validation evidence.

data_sources.label: Issue And CI History
data_sources.meaning: Project-management evidence needs defects, milestones, build pass rate, coverage, and flaky-test history.
data_sources.next_step: Add a connector/importer for issues, milestones, CI status, coverage, and release notes.

data_sources.label: AI Work Sessions
data_sources.meaning: AI session summaries show what an AI helped change, suggested, tested, or left uncertain.

measurement_plan.how_to_collect: Run fixed-rate proof scenarios at several observation counts and store every report.json.
measurement_plan.success_evidence: Throughput holds or improves across repeated matching runs with no failed required gates.
measurement_plan.title: Repeatable Ingest Load Curve
measurement_plan.why: Proves the system can accept field observations at useful rates without hidden bottlenecks.

measurement_plan.how_to_collect: Record per-endpoint p50/p95/p99 latency during proof and field-like runs.
measurement_plan.success_evidence: Query p95 is stable or improving, with endpoint-level evidence explaining outliers.

measurement_plan.why: Operator readiness depends on fast reads, not only successful ingest.

measurement_plan.how_to_collect: Run increasing observation counts and chart bytes per observation over time.
measurement_plan.success_evidence: DB bytes per observation stays flat or drops as sample size grows.
measurement_plan.title: Storage Growth Curve
measurement_plan.why: Storage cost must stay bounded as data volume grows beyond tiny demos.

measurement_plan.how_to_collect: Measure payload bytes, compressed bytes, first response, and full load time on a constrained link.
measurement_plan.success_evidence: Portal payload and load timing remain within the field-readiness threshold.

measurement_plan.why: Slow links and tunnels need payload and load-time evidence, not only local browser checks.

measurement_plan.how_to_collect: Import CI pass rate, failed proof runs, flaky tests, and repeated-regression counts.
measurement_plan.success_evidence: Failure rate trends down and high-priority defects do not repeatedly reappear.

measurement_plan.why: Repeated failures and flaky tests are project-management signals, not just engineering annoyances.

measurement_plan.how_to_collect: Capture review notes, field-test signoff, defect severity, and acceptance criteria.
measurement_plan.success_evidence: Each major claim has at least one human-reviewed acceptance record.

measurement_plan.why: The proof should connect to whether a real user or reviewer can trust the system.

measurement_plan.how_to_collect: Collect serial boot logs, sensor traces, flash/build result, and fault-injection notes.

measurement_plan.title: Firmware Boot And I/O Trace
measurement_plan.why: Firmware evidence is needed for devices that must start reliably and read sensors safely.

decision_rules: If a critical metric regresses, stabilize it before adding new features.
If a claim is thin, collect missing evidence before advertising improvement.
If a metric improves, add a regression guard before moving on.
If effort looks wasted, verify with issue, CI, or time-log data before changing priorities.

steps: Run the same scenario used by the comparable baseline.
Capture report.json and import it into BETA.

If it regresses again, profile the owning code path before adding new features.

later.why_now: Firmware evidence is needed for devices that must start reliably and read sensors safely.

entries.name: README.md
entries.note: Current BETA project-manager and AI analysis design notes
entries.sample_text:
# BETA

BETA means Build Environment for Testing & Analytics. It is a standalone evidence,
project-management, and improvement tracker for OMEGA and future software,
firmware, hardware, AI, data, and research projects. It reads proof `report.json`
files, project scans, issue/CI/bench/doc/AI-session inputs, keeps its own SQLite
history, analyzes progress, generates a local app, and can use Ollama as a local
AI analyst and project manager.

## Quick Start

The easiest way to work on the project is the local PowerShell helper:

```powershell
cd "C:\Users\jdcap\OneDrive\Documents\OMEGA Proof\omega-proof-lab"
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 serve
```

`dev.ps1` runs from the project root and supports:

```powershell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 doctor
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 test
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 refresh
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 ai -Model llama3.2:latest
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 ask -Model gemma4:latest -Question "Why are we behind and what should we test next?"
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 report
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 open
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 serve
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 status
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 stop
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 restart
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 init-project -ProjectName OMEGA -ProjectType mixed
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 ingest-info -ProjectKey omega -InfoPath C:\path\to\notes.md -SourceType ai-session
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 manage-ai -Model gemma4:latest
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 plan
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\dev.ps1 plan-ai -Model llama3.2:latest
```

If `serve` says th

entries.name: ai-analyst.md
entries.note: Current BETA project-manager and AI analysis design notes
entries.sample_text:
# AI Analyst Design

BETA uses AI as an engineering analyst and project-manager assistant layered on top of deterministic data.
The AI is not allowed to decide whether a metric improved or regressed. It receives
that comparison as an authoritative input and turns it into useful next steps.

## Local Model Strategy

- `--model auto-strong` asks Ollama for installed models and picks the largest one.
- A pinned model such as `qwen3.6:latest` can be used for consistent reports.
- Faster models can be used during iteration when speed matters more than depth.
- BETA sends `think: false` for structured JSON calls so thinking-capable
  models return the final answer in the response field instead of a separate
  reasoning trace.
- Malformed structured output is not accepted as an AI review. If a model returns
  a thought-only object, invalid schema, or times out, the dashboard keeps the
  deterministic analysis as the source of truth and marks the AI review unavailable.
- For large evidence payloads, pinning a practical local model such as
  `gemma4:latest` can be more useful than waiting on the largest installed model.
- For project-manager work, `manage --ai` asks the local model to review the
  operating plan, workflow health, project inputs, risks, and metric intelligence.

## Prompt Contract

The AI prompt includes:

- Summary metrics.
- Latest run metrics.
- Baseline run id.
- Metric-by-metric comparison facts.
- Claim evidence reasoning and caveats.
- Data lineage for latest and baseline proof reports.
- Deterministic findings.
- Deterministic improvement backlog.
- Connected and missing data sources.
- The current measurement plan.
- Deterministic work guidance for focus, avoid, and process-improvement advice.
- Deterministic operating plan with ranked actions, owner roles, success metrics,
  evidence requirements, and stop conditions.
- Metric intelligence with risk, confidence, volatility, streaks, and gap from best.
- Workflow health and project intelligence from

entries.name: project-manager.md
entries.note: Current BETA project-manager and AI analysis design notes
entries.sample_text:
# BETA Project Manager

BETA is meant to become a reusable project operating layer. The core loop is:

1. Initialize the project.
2. Scan the project files.
3. Import evidence and management inputs.
4. Generate the operating plan.
5. Ask the project-manager AI for advisory review.
6. Run tests or project work.
7. Import the new evidence and repeat.

## Project Setup

```powershell
python -m prooflab init-project --project-name OMEGA --project-type mixed --scan ..\OMEGA-wave
```

Supported project types:

- `software`
- `hardware`
- `firmware`
- `data`
- `ai`
- `research`
- `mixed`

The setup writes `project.json` with project goals, metric families, evidence
sources, management cadence, setup checklist, and AI collaboration rules.

## Information Ingestion

```powershell
python -m prooflab ingest-info omega C:\path\to\issues.csv --source-type issues
python -m prooflab ingest-info omega C:\path\to\ci-results.json --source-type ci
python -m prooflab ingest-info omega C:\path\to\codex-summary.md --source-type ai-session
python -m prooflab ingest-info omega C:\path\to\bench-log.csv --source-type bench
```

`ingest-info` does not need every source to use the same schema yet. It creates a
manifest with file metadata, row counts, JSON keys, and short text samples. The
manager plan uses those manifests to know which evidence sources are connected.

## Project Manager Brief

```powershell
python -m prooflab manage
python -m prooflab manage --ai --model gemma4:latest
```

The deterministic manager brief includes:

- Current priorities from the operating plan.
- Risks from critical metrics and missing sources.
- Next checkpoints.
- Data sources to connect.
- Planned metrics that should become active.
- Workflow levers.
- AI collaboration guidance.

The AI manager review is advisory. It should improve the work plan, but the
evidence still comes from metrics, reports, project scans, and imported inputs.

## AI Collaboration Data

When an AI helps with project work, save a short

last_import.note: Current BETA project-manager and AI analysis design notes

metric_catalog.reason: Measures whether the product does what it claims.
Tracks efficiency across software and hardware.

recommended_next_artifacts: claims.md: claims, acceptance thresholds, and evidence source for each claim
metrics.json: stable metric ids, units, higher/lower-is-better, and thresholds
scenarios/*.json: repeatable software, firmware, or bench scenarios
reports/<run-id>/report.json: machine-readable results for every run

test_matrix.test: Map each public/internal claim to one measurable metric and one acceptance threshold.

test_matrix.automation: CLI, integration test, or hardware-in-loop script
test_matrix.evidence: report.json, logs, screenshots, or bench sheet
test_matrix.test: Run the smallest end-to-end scenario that proves the build can complete its main job.
Repeat the same scenario after every meaningful change and compare to the previous matching run.

test_matrix.automation: load test or extended bench run
test_matrix.test: Increase load, duration, dataset size, or duty cycle until a limit is observed.

test_matrix.evidence: photos, measurements, serial logs, and pass/fail sheet
test_matrix.test: Run a bench checklist for power, thermal, mechanical fit, firmware boot, and I/O behavior.

avoid.better_option: Use real project runs and keep the quarantined list as an audit trail.
avoid.reason: Quarantined local/demo reports can be useful for UI testing, but they cannot prove project progress.
avoid.title: Do not use demo data as proof

avoid.better_option: Repeat the same scenario after each meaningful change and compare against the matching baseline.
avoid.reason: A single run can be noise; the dashboard needs repeated comparable runs before calling progress real.
avoid.title: Do not chase one-off numbers

avoid.better_option: Import issue, CI, benchmark, or work-log data before making hard claims about wasted time.
avoid.reason: Time-spend and project-management claims are currently inferred, not directly imported.
avoid.title: Do not optimize unmeasured work

work_guidance.basis: Deterministic guidance from metric comparisons, QA matrix, effort focus, data-quality checks, and measurement plan.
decision_rules: If a critical metric regresses, stabilize it before adding new features.
If a claim is thin, collect missing evidence before advertising improvement.
If a metric improves, add a regression guard before moving on.
If effort looks wasted, verify with issue, CI, or time-log data before changing priorities.

focus_now.next_step: Run a real bench, field, CI, or hardware validation and import its report.json into this project.
focus_now.reason: The current imported reports are not accepted as real proof data, so progress and improvement claims would be misleading.
focus_now.title: Collect real evidence before optimizing

focus_now.next_step: Run fixed-rate proof scenarios at several observation counts and store every report.json.
focus_now.reason: Proves the system can accept field observations at useful rates without hidden bottlenecks.
focus_now.success_metric: Throughput holds or improves across repeated matching runs with no failed required gates.
focus_now.title: Repeatable Ingest Load Curve

focus_now.next_step: Record per-endpoint p50/p95/p99 latency during proof and field-like runs.
focus_now.reason: Operator readiness depends on fast reads, not only successful ingest.
focus_now.success_metric: Query p95 is stable or improving, with endpoint-level evidence explaining outliers.
focus_now.title: Endpoint Latency Distribution

focus_now.next_step: Run increasing observation counts and chart bytes per observation over time.
focus_now.reason: Storage cost must stay bounded as data volume grows beyond tiny demos.
focus_now.success_metric: DB bytes per observation stays flat or drops as sample size grows.
focus_now.title: Storage Growth Curve

focus_now.next_step: Collect serial boot logs, sensor traces, flash/build result, and fault-injection notes.
focus_now.reason: Firmware evidence is needed for devices that must start reliably and read sensors safely.
focus_now.success_metric: Firmware boots repeatedly and reports expected I/O without unsafe failures.
focus_now.title: Firmware Boot And I/O Trace

next_ai_questions: What is the single highest leverage test to run next?
Which metric regression most weakens the claims?
What should we stop doing until the evidence improves?
What data source would most improve confidence?
work_guidance.summary: Use the current proof data to choose the next narrow improvement loop.

work_better.habit: Plan one change, run one matching proof scenario, save the report, then review the regression and QA matrix.
work_better.title: Use a tight evidence loop
work_better.why: This makes progress attributable instead of mixing several changes into one unclear result.

work_better.habit: Add metrics as structured report fields instead of notes whenever possible.
work_better.title: Keep proof reports small but complete
work_better.why: Structured data feeds charts, AI review, reports, and trend analysis automatically.

work_better.habit: Use current values to prove presence, but use matching baselines and repeated runs to prove improvement.
work_better.title: Separate current-state proof from improvement proof
work_better.why: This prevents the tool from overstating progress when it only has a snapshot.

work_better.habit: Start with: Real proof report is available.
work_better.title: Close data-quality gaps before polishing
work_better.why: Accepted real reports: 0; quarantined demo/synthetic reports: 6.

improvement_levers: Increase repeat depth to at least three matching runs per important scenario.
Connect issue/CI, bench, field, or work-log sources so project-management claims stop being inferred.
Promote planned metrics into active report fields.
Close data-quality gaps before treating improvements as durable.
Run a local AI review after deterministic analysis has current data.
workflow_health.label: thin
